name: Zadar Old Town (Sea Organ + Sun Salutation)
description: Trip starting point. Old town peninsula with the Sea Organ and Greeting to the Sun at sunset — Hitchcock called it the best sunset in the world.
communityNotes: "Sea Organ at sunset is genuinely one of the most peaceful things on the trip — the sounds are random and beautiful." Alfred Hitchcock allegedly called the Zadar sunset the most beautiful in the world. Sun Salutation (giant solar-powered light disk) activates at night right next to the Organ — stay for dusk. Old town is a 5-minute walk from the ferry port. Parking outside walls, walk in. Maraschino cherry liqueur is the local speciality. Not crowded at 10:00 on Day 1.

name: Plitvice Lakes National Park
description: 16 terraced turquoise lakes with waterfalls. ~1.5h north of Zadar — only realistic as a day one/two detour before heading south.
bestTime: enter at 07:00 or after 16:00 to dodge tour buses
communityNotes: Plitvice rejected: +4h drive in wrong direction, swimming banned, 36pp boardwalk. Paklenica is the gorge fix on the route.

name: Krka National Park (Skradinski Buk)
description: Waterfall cascades between Zadar and Split. Swimming below the falls is no longer allowed, but the boardwalk loop is short and spectacular.
communityNotes: ⚠️ REJECTED: Swimming banned since 2021. €40pp for a boardwalk is bad value for this group. Kravica Falls (Bosnia, Day 6) delivers the waterfall swimming experience at €5-8pp. Recent 2024-2026 reviews confirm it's not worth it without swimming.

name: Paklenica National Park
description: Velebit canyon park ~45min from Zadar: serious hiking and one of Europe's best-known climbing spots. Velika Paklenica gorge walk to the mountain hut is the classic.
communityNotes: "Paklenica gorge walk feels like a slot canyon for the first few km — sheer 300m walls." Big Paklenica (Velika Paklenica) starts at the Starigrad carpark. Free entry to the gorge approach; the deeper NP costs €10pp. Climbing: Anića Kuk is one of Croatia's famous rock faces. The walk to Manita Peć cave (1.5-2h RT) is the most-praised hike for non-climbers. Night sky: Jun 16-18 is new moon window — Cape Planka visible from the Paklenica coast if camping nearby.

name: Telašćica Nature Park (Dugi Otok)
description: Cliffs, salt lake and bays on Dugi Otok island, reachable by ferry from Zadar. Candidate for the 'island day' if we take the car ferry.
communityNotes: "Telašćica is what Plitvice would be if it were by the sea — dramatic cliffs, a saltwater lake warm enough to swim in, stunning." (travel forums). The saltwater Mir lake is the main draw: 2°C warmer than the sea, very calm. Day-trip by boat from Sali (Dugi Otok) or from Zadar by charter. If you're in Zadar on Day 1, this could be a boat-day from Sali (but it adds a ferry to Dugi Otok). More realistically a Zadar area boat option if the group has the energy. Entrance to the nature park: ~€5-10pp.

name: Split (Diocletian's Palace)
description: Roman palace turned living old town. Also the main ferry hub for Hvar/Brač/Vis and good nightlife.
communityNotes: "Diocletian's Palace is a living city — apartments and restaurants built into 1,700-year-old Roman walls." Meštrović Gallery is worthwhile if time allows. The Riva seafront promenade is good for an hour in the evening but thick with tourists. Best kept short (1-2h transit stop). Parking: best at the port garage, expensive. Krka NP: skip — €40pp boardwalk, no swimming since 2021, bad ratio.

name: Omiš (Cetina canyon)
description: Small town at the mouth of the Cetina river canyon — pebble beach meets sheer cliff walls. Base for extreme sports: canyoning (€70pp), zipline over the Cetina (€80pp, one of Croatia's longest), river rafting, and kayaking. Cetina river swimming holes are free and cold. Day 2 hub activity.
communityNotes: "Extreme canyoning was the best thing we did in Croatia — 4h of jumping, abseiling, swimming through gorges." (Reddit r/croatia, 2024). Zipline: 150m height, 1.3km long, views over the canyon and sea. Must book 1-2 days ahead in June. River Cetina swim (free): find the pull-off near Radmanove Mlinice — emerald-green, cold, beautiful. "Omiš felt like one of Croatia's best-kept secrets — touristy but not wrecked." Camp Lisičina nearby is the obvious night before activity.

name: Biokovo Skywalk (Makarska)
description: Glass-bottomed viewing platform 1,228m above the Adriatic on the Biokovo NP road — hanging out over the edge with sea and islands below. On the same toll road as Sveti Jure summit drive. Car slot must be pre-booked online (sells out in June).
communityNotes: "The drive up alone is stunning — you emerge above the clouds." Pre-book the car slot online: biokovo.hrvi.hr (max 300 cars/day, fills ahead). Skywalk entry ~€15pp on top of NP entry (~€10). Open 08:00-19:00. Arrive early — the afternoon haze reduces the views. Best June strategy: pre-book Day 3 morning, leave Makarska by 08:00 to reach Skywalk before the crowd.
cost: ~€10pp NP entry + ~€15pp Skywalk + parking; car slot booking mandatory

name: Hvar Town
description: Island town known for beaches, the Spanish fortress and the party scene (Hula Hula, Carpe Diem). Car ferry from Split (Stari Grad) or Drvenik (Sućuraj).
communityNotes: "Hvar is beautiful but has become a party destination for a reason — it's also expensive." The old town itself is lovely (Fortica fortress views, loggia square, harbor). Lavender fields June–July. The bus to Stari Grad for the ferry is separate from the car ferry. Verdict for this route: almost certainly doesn't fit Days 1-3 pre-pickup (it requires ferry from Split then you're stranded on the island). Post-pickup backtracking to Hvar is poor value. Kept as candidate in case the group finds an unexpected day.

name: Dubrovnik Old Town
description: Trip end point — car drop-off and bus/flight back to Vienna on Jun 28. Walls walk, Srđ cable car/viewpoint, Buža cliff bar.
bestTime: Pre-08:00 or after 19:00; avoid 10:00–17:00 in peak season
communityNotes: "The walls are best before 08:00 — by 09:30 they're shoulder-to-shoulder with cruise tourists." Wall entry ~€35pp. Old town itself free to walk. Lokrum boat from the old harbor (10min, €15-20rt) is a great half-day — peacocks, saltwater lake, Game of Thrones ruins. Elaphiti islands: day-trip boat from Gruž harbor. Game of Thrones filming locations scattered throughout. Evening old town after 20:00 is the best vibe — most day-trippers gone.

name: Mostar (Stari Most)
description: Ottoman old town around the rebuilt 16th-century bridge; locals dive 24m into the Neretva. Main Bosnia base candidate.
bestTime: Arrive late afternoon; evening and morning are the magic hours
communityNotes: "The old town bazaar near the bridge is incredibly atmospheric — feel the same street as 500 years ago, minus the tourists." Divers jump from Stari Most daily at ~14:00 (can be €20-30 for a dive by young locals showing off). Kujundžiluk bazaar street is very commercialized but worth walking once. Evening walk after 20:00: bridge lit, few tourists left, genuinely beautiful. 'Black Dog' bar is the local/traveler hangout. Blagaj Tekija (whirling dervish tekke at a spring, 15min south) is a must-add — often skipped and absolutely worth it.

name: Blagaj Tekija
description: Dervish monastery at the cliff spring of the Buna river, 20min from Mostar. Spring is one of the strongest karst springs in Europe. Knees/shoulders covered to enter the tekija (wraps provided free). Park at the lower lots — the final street is a midday tour-bus trap.
cost: 10 KM / €5 cash to enter the tekija
communityNotes: "One of the most atmospheric places in the whole Balkans — the tekija building literally sits on the karst spring with emerald water gushing out below it." 20 min south of Mostar. Entrance ~€2pp. Combine with Mostar Day 6 morning (drive, park below, 30–45min). The Buna spring gushes 43m³/sec — the blue-green water color is surreal. Tekija (dervish lodge) still active; remove shoes before entering. Summer: warm enough for a dip in the pool below. "Worth every minute; felt like a fairytale."

name: Kravica Waterfalls
description: 25m horseshoe waterfall arc on the Trebižat river — you can swim directly in the plunge pool below the falls. ~40min from Mostar, on the route to/from Croatia. June gets busy but manageable if you arrive early. Stunning emerald-green water.
bestTime: arrive ~07:00–08:00 to beat the crowds; June pool swimmable (~18–20°C)
communityNotes: Arrive 07:00–09:00 before tour buses arrive (~10:30 is the cutoff). Entry €10/adult (cash preferred). "Swimming below the falls is incredible — feels like a tropical paradise." Slippery rocks — water shoes strongly recommended. Summer afternoons: loud music, crowded platforms, very different vibe. | Early entry = empty falls + better photos.

name: Počitelj
description: Ottoman hillside fortress village on the Neretva, basically on the Mostar–coast road. Quick climb to the tower for the view. Free 30-min stop if passing at golden hour.
communityNotes: "The village is only a 10-min stop but the climb to the Gavankula tower gives a great view over the Neretva. Worth it if passing." Literally on the road between coast and Mostar — no detour. Ottoman clock tower + mosque + old houses. Entrance free (tower may charge €1-2). "It's small but very picturesque — the contrast of the pale stone against the Neretva below." Combine with Kravica in the same morning (Kravica at 07:00, Počitelj 09:30, Mostar by 11:00).

name: Trebinje
description: Relaxed Herzegovinian wine town 30min inland from Dubrovnik — locals' favorite cheap base near Dubrovnik. Candidate for the last nights (much cheaper than Dubrovnik).
communityNotes: "The most underrated old town in the Balkans — a real Balkan city with almost no tourists." The stari grad (walled old town, Arslanagić bridge) is 20 min walk from anywhere. Tvrdoš Monastery with its winery (Vranac wine, €3 a glass in the cellar shop) is a 20-minute drive. Hercegovina Gračanica mosque above town gives a view over the valley. Very hot in June (35°C+). Republika Srpska entity — Cyrillic signage, Orthodox flavor, completely different from Mostar or Sarajevo. Good €35/night Airbnb (Gaga City Center). Half-day is enough; good transit stop Day 3-4 and the Day 12 overnight before Dubrovnik.

name: Dubrovnik Airport (Čilipi) — friend pickup + car drop-off
description: TWO HARD ANCHORS here (20km S of Dubrovnik, Konavle): (1) pick up the 4th person Jun 18 evening; (2) DROP THE RENTAL CAR by 20:00 on Jun 28 (Sicily By Car). Days 1–3 must end near here; the loop returns here at the end. Full day usable on Jun 28 before the 20:00 drop.

name: Zadar Airport (ZAD) — car pickup Jun 16 10:00
description: TRIP START: rental car (Kia Sportage or similar, Sicily By Car) ready at 10:00 on Jun 16, after arrival flight FR4807. 10km SE of Zadar old town. Day 1 effectively starts here ~10:30. See research/rental-car.md.

name: Sutjeska National Park (Tjentište)
description: Bosnia's oldest national park, on our exit route east towards the Šćepan Polje border: Maglić (BiH's highest peak), Perućica primeval forest, dramatic Tjentište valley with the brutalist WWII monument. Fits the 'deeper into the Bosnian mountains' wish on a transit day.
communityNotes: "Tjentište memorial is genuinely moving — one of Yugoslavia's great monuments. Dramatic location between mountains." Perućica primeval forest requires pre-booking: max 16pp/day, guided only, ~€38pp (call +387 58 233-130). Skakavac waterfall visible from Dragoš Sedlo viewpoint without a guide. The macadam road to Dragoš Sedlo is doable in a regular car (5km, 1.5h stop total). Given the Day 8 time pressure (Tara rafting), Sutjeska fits best as a quick transit stop: Tjentište monument (30min, on-road) + Dragoš Sedlo (1.5h detour) if arriving Šćepan Polje by 13:00.
